sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Napoli have shown strong attacking form in their last 5 matches, winning 3 and scoring 9 goals, despite some defensive lapses with no clean sheets. Genoa, on the other hand, are struggling, with only 1 win in their last 5 and just 5 goals scored, while conceding 8. Napoli's superior league position (4th) compared to Genoa (19th) further highlights their dominance.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last 2 head-to-head matches, Napoli have been unbeaten, with a win (2-1) and a draw (2-2). Both games saw goals from both teams, indicating a trend of competitive, high-scoring encounters. Napoli's historical edge (2 wins, 3 draws in last 5 matchups) supports their favoritism.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Napoli's stats show dominance in possession (60.6%) and goal-scoring (10 goals, 3rd in league), with strong rankings in big chances created and penalty area entries. Genoa's poor standing (19th) and low goal tally (2 goals) reflect their struggles offensively and defensively. These metrics underline Napoli's likelihood of controlling the game.

sections.subtitle: Key Trends and Insights
sections.text: Trends indicate over 2.5 goals in recent Napoli matches and head-to-heads, alongside both teams scoring in most encounters. Genoa often concede first and struggle to keep clean sheets (none in last 9 against Napoli). Napoli's high corner count (6.4 per game) and Genoa's low corner stats suggest set-piece opportunities for the hosts.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Genoa leads Napoli 1-0 at the Stadio Diego Armando Maradona. Jeff Ekhator's goal in the 33rd minute has given the away side the edge despite Napoli's dominance in possession and territory. The home team has struggled to convert their control into clear scoring opportunities, while Genoa capitalized on a key moment to take the lead. The first half showcased a stark contrast in efficiency between the two sides.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Napoli's performance
halftimeSections.text: Napoli has controlled the game for large periods, dominating ball possession and frequently entering the final third. Despite creating a big chance, they failed to convert, with a notable miss proving costly. Their aerial and ground duels have been highly successful, showing physical superiority. However, the lack of shots on target is a major concern, indicating a need for sharper finishing or better shot selection in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Genoa's performance
halftimeSections.text: Genoa has been far less involved in possession but made the most of their limited opportunities, with Ekhator's goal coming from a well-executed chance. Their defensive structure has held firm against Napoli’s pressure, though they’ve struggled in duels and aerial challenges. Discipline is an issue with a couple of yellow cards picked up, which could impact their approach in the second half. Their ability to win possession in the final third has been a bright spot.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half began with Napoli asserting early dominance, pushing forward with consistent attacking intent. Genoa had brief spells of momentum, particularly around the time of their goal, where they shifted the pressure onto Napoli’s defense. Napoli regained control for extended periods, maintaining a high level of threat, but Genoa mounted a strong surge late in the half, showing resilience and finishing the period on the front foot.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Napoli, Matteo Politano stands out as a potential game-changer in the second half, having been involved in dangerous positions despite missing a big chance. David Neres also showed creativity and could be pivotal in unlocking Genoa’s defense. On the Genoa side, Jeff Ekhator remains a threat after his first-half goal, while Brooke Norton-Cuffy’s contribution in creating opportunities highlights his importance to their attack.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Napoli is likely to come out aggressively in the second half, pushing for an equalizer with their superior possession and territorial advantage. They will need to be more clinical in front of goal to turn their dominance into results. Genoa, on the other hand, may focus on defending their lead, relying on counter-attacks to exploit any gaps left by Napoli’s forward push. Discipline will be key for Genoa to avoid further bookings or potential dismissals.
